CheckTitle: Service principals with privileged Entra directory roles must have no owners
Description: Service principals holding privileged Microsoft Entra directory roles (such as Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator) are evaluated for the presence of owners on either the service principal itself or its parent app registration.
Risk: An owner of a service principal or its parent app registration can rotate credentials, add new secrets, and sign in as the service principal — inheriting its privileged directory role entirely outside PIM approval flows and Conditional Access policies targeting user accounts. Microsoft documents this as a known privilege escalation path.

Code.Other:
1. Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center (https://entra.microsoft.com)
2. Go to Identity > Applications > Enterprise applications > select the service principal
3. Under Owners, remove all owners
4. Repeat for the parent App Registration under Identity > Applications > App registrations
5. Use PIM eligible assignments instead of permanent role assignments where possible

Recommendation.Text: Remove all owners from service principals that hold privileged Entra directory roles. Manage privileged service principals exclusively via PIM-eligible role assignments and break-glass controls. Ensure no human account can silently inherit control-plane privileges through ownership.

Notes: Only service principals with permanent Tier 0 directory role assignments are evaluated. Microsoft first-party service principals and disabled service principals are excluded.
